steps.title: Spot the key phrase
steps.content: The second sentence says "Duchamp's Fountain did just that" — the work actually accomplished what Duchamp intended in sentence 1. The transition must confirm that the intention was realized.

steps.title: Match the confirmation
steps.content:
The logical relationship is concession/confirmation (emphatic affirmation). Sentence 1 states Duchamp's intent; sentence 2 confirms that intent was fully realized with "did just that."
  • Choice A — "Indeed,": signals emphatic confirmation — the second sentence affirms the first with force, so fits. ✓
  • Choice B — "Similarly,": signals parallel comparison to a separate example, but there is no second example — just confirmation of the same claim, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "Instead,": signals contrast or substitution, implying the work did something other than what Duchamp intended — contradicts "did just that," so fails. ✗
  • Choice D — "In addition,": signals an extra, unrelated point being appended, so fails. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
